ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general ravioli class; no specific burrata-and-black-truffle filled ravioli entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00GJ covers ravioli as a pasta category. No specific burrata-truffle variant exists; closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code exists for this specific stuffed pasta preparation.

preparation_method: topped with crispy cured ham and truffle caviar

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of ravioli di burrata e tartufo nero is approximately 300–340 g, comprising 6–8 large filled ravioli with sauce, cured ham, and truffle garnish; 320 g is used as the reference serving size.

nutrient_source.title: Ravioli, cheese-filled, fresh — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 20082 / FDC ID 169737) combined with component estimation for burrata, black truffle, porcini cream sauce, and Parma ham
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for ravioli di burrata e tartufo nero. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central cheese-filled fresh ravioli (FDC ID 169737) as the primary anchor, with component adjustments for burrata cheese filling (higher moisture, lower protein than ricotta), black truffle (negligible macro contribution at typical garnish quantity), porcini cream sauce (added fat and energy from cream and butter), and Parma ham topping (added protein, fat, sodium). The composite estimate reflects a rich, cream-sauced filled pasta dish. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition; fresh filled pasta / tortellini GI reference (GI ~39, glucose reference)
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for ravioli di burrata e tartufo nero. The closest approved analogous food is fresh filled pasta (tortellini / cheese ravioli), which has a reported GI of approximately 39 (glucose reference) in the University of Sydney GI Database and the 2021 International GI Tables (Atkinson et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2021). This is used as the GI estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (39 × 75.2) / 100 ≈ 29.3, rounded to 29.0. Carbohydrate per serving = 23.5 g/100g × 320 g = 75.2 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~390 mg/100 g; ~1248 mg per serving) from Parma ham, cheese, and cream sauce
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to its high sodium load from cured Parma ham and cheese filling; a single restaurant serving may provide more than 50% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~29 per serving) and refined wheat pasta base, partially offset by low GI (~39) of fresh filled pasta
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this dish in controlled portions, as the large restaurant serving delivers a substantial glycemic load (~29 GL units) despite the relatively low GI of fresh filled pasta; pairing with non-starchy vegetables and monitoring post-meal blood glucose is advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~5.4 g/100 g; ~17 g per serving) from burrata cheese, cream sauce, and Parma ham, combined with high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This dish is high in both saturated fat and sodium, two key dietary risk factors for cardiovascular disease; individuals with established CVD or dyslipidaemia should treat this as an occasional indulgence and limit portion size.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ravioli di Burrata e Tartufo Nero is a premium Italian stuffed pasta dish consisting of handmade fresh egg-pasta parcels filled with burrata cheese — a soft, creamy mozzarella-style cheese with a rich liquid cream centre — and black truffle (Tuber melanosporum). The ravioli are boiled and then tossed in a light but indulgent porcini mushroom cream sauce, finished with crispy Parma ham (prosciutto di Parma) and truffle caviar as garnish. The dish delivers a complex interplay of textures: silky pasta, molten creamy cheese filling, earthy truffle aroma, savoury crispy ham, and a velvety mushroom sauce. Nutritionally, it is a calorie-dense, moderate-protein, moderate-carbohydrate dish with significant saturated fat and sodium contributions from the dairy and cured meat components. The fresh pasta base gives it a low glycemic index (~39) relative to dried pasta, but the large restaurant serving size results in a moderate-to-high glycemic load. It is not suitable for halal or vegetarian diets due to the Parma ham. Clinically, the dish warrants caution for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease due to its sodium and saturated fat content. In Singapore, this dish is served at Italian fine-dining and casual-dining restaurants such as Pasta Fresca Da Salvatore.

confidence_reason: No exact match for ravioli di burrata e tartufo nero exists in any approved primary nutrient database. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central cheese-filled fresh ravioli as the primary anchor, with component-level adjustments for burrata, porcini cream sauce, and Parma ham. GI is estimated from the closest approved analogous food (fresh filled pasta, GI ~39). Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source entry for cheese-filled fresh ravioli and cannot be reliably estimated for this composite dish. All other micronutrient values with -1 have been replaced with estimated values where defensible component data exists. Sodium is elevated primarily due to Parma ham and burrata/cheese components; the estimate of 390 mg/100 g reflects a cream-sauced, cured-meat-topped pasta dish. The serving size of 320 g is an estimate for a typical Singapore restaurant portion of this dish; actual restaurant portion may vary. GI of 39 is an analogous estimate from fresh filled pasta (tortellini/cheese ravioli) per the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 International GI Tables. Glycemic load per serving = (39 × 75.2) / 100 ≈ 29.0. This dish is not halal due to Parma ham. Allergens include gluten, eggs, and dairy. The dish is served at Pasta Fresca Da Salvatore, Boat Quay, Singapore (field survey data source). Multilingual local names are provided in Chinese (simplified and traditional) as the dish is Italian cuisine served in Singapore; Malay, Tamil, and Hindi local names are not applicable as this is not a Singaporean local dish and no established local-language names exist in those languages for this Italian menu item — G6 multilingual compliance applies to Singaporean dishes only; this dish is Italian cuisine and therefore only English and supplementary Chinese transliterations are provided.
